title: olearia ramulosa var. microphylla
intro: Olearia ramulosa var. microphylla, also known as Dwarf Tree Daisy or Small-leaved Olearia, is a variety of shrub-like plant native to Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and compact growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Olearia ramulosa var. microphylla include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce fever, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its leaves and flowers being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
